headline_drivers: FQ2/FY25 earnings print with improved profitability and positive net income (net income ~$6.3M vs loss prior quarter), alongside FY revenue run-rate in the ~$2.7B range and maintained path to profitability (reported in early May 2026)

Order momentum and backlog strength: management commentary around record contracted backlog in the mid‑single‑digit billions of dollars and year‑to‑date order intake roughly doubling YoY, with ~50% of new orders from new customers, signaling sustained demand for utility-scale storage

Data-center / hyperscaler growth narrative: company discussion of rapidly expanding data-center storage pipeline (double‑digit GWh, up sharply QoQ) and MSAs signed with two major hyperscalers, with first purchase order expected in the near term, fueling an AI/data‑center–infrastructure re‑rating

Sector and sentiment tailwinds: investors rotating into grid-scale storage and AI‑power infrastructure names following stronger demand commentary across power/energy‑storage peers, plus a high‑short‑interest squeeze/technical breakout after a multi‑month trough and prior 52‑week low near $4–5
summary: The big move in FLNC over the last week is primarily a re‑rating off its early‑May earnings and backlog narrative, not a random squeeze. The company showed a clean inflection toward profitability (swinging to positive net income with improving margins), confirmed a multi‑billion‑dollar contracted backlog and sharply higher order intake, and highlighted a fast‑growing data‑center/hyperscaler pipeline that ties it directly into the AI power build‑out. That combination – proof the business model is working, visibility on multi‑year demand, and an emerging hyperscaler angle – triggered aggressive buying in a name that had been heavily sold and volatile, amplifying the move via short covering and technical breakout behavior on 1.7x+ normal volume.
